finding: A workflow-captured provenance start can be structurally trusted yet stale enough to describe an unrelated or reused execution window. Deployment-proof provenance now fails closed when the claimed build duration exceeds six hours.
design_implication: Cloudflare deployment evidence chronology is bounded before an in-toto statement is emitted; untrusted start sources still collapse to the observed finish, future starts are rejected, and trusted-but-stale starts are rejected.

verification.verified_behavior: The exact committed implementation rejects finishedOn - startedOn greater than 21600000 milliseconds with startedOn-exceeds-maximum-duration.

falsification_route: Run node --test cloudflare-static/attest-deployment-proof.test.mjs in GitHub Actions or a fresh checkout. The new adversarial case must fail if a workflow-captured start is six hours and one minute before finish, while the one-minute case must pass.
privacy_boundary: No Cloudflare credentials, token values, private chat, health data, user-agent strings, or personal identifiers are stored.
limits: The six-hour ceiling is a defensive operational bound, not a measured Cloudflare maximum.
No workflow run or Cloudflare deployment was observed for the test commit.
This protects provenance chronology; it does not prove the deployment or scientific claims are true.
